title: Ensure that 'Guest invite restrictions' is set to 'Only users  assigned to specific admin roles [...]' or 'No one [..]'

input_row.title: Ensure that 'Guest invite restrictions' is set to 'Only users  assigned to specific admin roles [...]' or 'No one [..]'
input_row.description: Restrict invitations to either users with specific administrative roles or no one.
input_row.rationale: Restricting invitations to users with specific administrator roles ensures that only authorized accounts have access to cloud resources. This helps to maintain "Need to Know" permissions and prevents inadvertent access to data. By default the setting Guest invite restrictions is set to Anyone in the organization can invite guest users including guests and non-admins. This would allow anyone within the organization to invite guests and non-admins to the tenant, posing a security risk.
input_row.audit: Note: This setting has 4 levels of restriction, which include: • Anyone in the organization can invite guest users including guests and non- admins (most inclusive), • Member users and users assigned to specific admin roles can invite guest users including guests with member permissions, • Only users assigned to specific admin roles can invite guest users, • No one in the organization can invite guest users including admins (most restrictive). Audit from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID 3. Under Manage, select External Identities 4. Select External collaboration settings 5. Under Guest invite settings, ensure that Guest invite restrictions is set to either Only users assigned to specific admin roles can invite guest users or No one in the organization [...] Audit from Powershell Enter the following: Connect-MgGraph (Get-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy).AllowInvitesFrom If the resulting value is adminsAndGuestInviters or none the configuration complies.
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID 3. Under Manage, select External Identities 4. Select External collaboration settings 5. Under Guest invite settings, set Guest invite restrictions to either Only users assigned to specific admin roles can invite guest users or No one in the organization [...] 6. Click Save Remediate from Powershell Enter the following: Connect-MgGraph Update-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy -AllowInvitesFrom "adminsAndGuestInviters" Alternatively, to set this to the most restrictive No one in the organization [...] enter the following: Connect-MgGraph Update-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy -AllowInvitesFrom "none"
input_row.impact: With the option of Only users assigned to specific admin roles can invite guest users selected, users with specific admin roles will be in charge of sending invitations to the external users, requiring additional overhead by them to manage user accounts. This will mean coordinating with other departments as they are onboarding new users.
input_row.default_value: By default, Guest invite restrictions is set to Anyone in the organization can invite guest users including guests and non-admins
input_row.justification: The control in question is specific to Microsoft Azure's guest invite restrictions, which do not have a direct equivalent in AWS IAM. AWS IAM does not support a built-in concept of "guest invites" as Azure AD does. Therefore, the control cannot be directly automated using AWS APIs or CLI. The first AI opinion's suggestion to automate using AWS IAM policies is not applicable since it attempts to simulate a control that does not exist in AWS.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Log in to the Azure Portal. 2. Navigate to Microsoft Entra ID from the Azure Home. 3. Under the "Manage" section, select "External Identities." 4. Click on "External collaboration settings." 5. Verify that the "Guest invite restrictions" setting is configured to either "Only users assigned to specific admin roles can invite guest users" or "No one in the organization can invite guest users." 6. Ensure the setting is saved and documented for compliance records.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_entra_id_guest_invite_restrictions_configuration

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Utilize the Microsoft Graph API to check the guest invite restrictions settings.
- Use the Azure SDK for Python to interact with the Microsoft Graph API or PowerShell commands as referenced in the control.
- The specific Azure CLI command to verify guest invite restrictions is:
  ```
  Connect-MgGraph
  Get-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy | Select-Object AllowInvitesFrom
  ```
- Alternatively, the PowerShell command could also be used directly to get the same results:
  ```
  Connect-MgGraph
  (Get-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy).AllowInvitesFrom
  ```

- Enumerate targeted Azure resources, specifically focusing on "Microsoft Entra ID" settings related to guest invites.
- Configuration properties to validate include:
  - The `AllowInvitesFrom` property of the `PolicyAuthorizationPolicy`.
  
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:
  - **Pass**: The `AllowInvitesFrom` value is either "adminsAndGuestInviters" or "none".
  - **Fail**: Any other value indicates non-compliance, such as "anyone".

- In summary, this program will query the Microsoft Graph API's policy settings to check if the guest invite restrictions comply with the specified standards outlined in the CIS control.
